raw_consumer_statement: I had a dispute with a merchant closed by American Express recently and I called them today to ask them to reopen it. I explained that I purchased items that were very much not as described ( the material was so thin it was see through, the seams were misaligned, etc ) and according to the merchant 's website that I bought it from, they would provide shipping labels for all quality related issues. The woman from the dispute team I spoke to at American Express told me she couldn't reopen the claim as it went against the merchant 's policies. I asked her what she meant since the merchant 's website clearly stated they would cover shipping for quality issues but when I reached out to them they refused to and wasn't the purpose of American Express to protect their clients in these instances? She just kept repeating that she couldn't go against the merchants policies despite my telling her I HAD the merchant 's return policy open on my computer at that moment and it VERY clearly outlines they DO provide return shipping labels for quality issues. I then asked her if she was going to make the merchant uphold their own return policies and she again stated that she couldn't do anything. At this point, I'm reconsidering why we even have American Express if they're not going to protect us in situations like this. I don't know if the woman I spoke to from the dispute team was indicative of the broader employee base or is a unique anomaly but given that I spend roughly $ XXXX with American Express monthly between the cards I have with them, and have an issue MAYBE once a year, I expect them to handle it when I do. If they can't, I don't need to stay with them.
